testcase.name: runScenario["Verify WordPress website functionalities", "WordPress Website Automation"](1)

testcase.system-out: Scenario: Verify WordPress website functionalities # src/test/resources/features/wordPress.feature:3 Given User launches WordPress website # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.launchWebsite() Then Verify the page title # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.verifyTitle() When User clicks on Download and Get WordPress # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.clickDownload() Then Verify middle text as "Get WordPress" # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.verifyMiddleText(java.lang.String) When User clicks Community and Photo Directory # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.clickCommunity() And User searches for "nature" # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.searchPic(java.lang.String) Then Verify pictures are displayed # stepDefinitions.WordPressSteps.verifyPictures() org.openqa.selenium.StaleElementReferenceException: stale element reference: stale element not found (Session info: chrome=145.0.7632.159) For SLF4J: Defaulting to no-operation (NOP) logger implementation SLF4J: See http://www.slf4j.org/codes.html#StaticLoggerBinder for further details. Mar 04, 2026 4:35:36 PM org.openqa.selenium.devtools.CdpVersionFinder findNearestMatch WARNING: Unable to find CDP implementation matching 145 Mar 04, 2026 4:35:36 PM org.openqa.selenium.chromium.ChromiumDriver lambda$new$5 WARNING: Unable to find version of CDP to use for 145.0.7632.159. You may need to include a dependency on a specific version of the CDP using something similar to `org.seleniumhq.selenium:selenium-devtools-v86:4.18.1` where the version ("v86") matches the version of the chromium-based browser you're using and the version number of the artifact is the same as Selenium's.
